summary: Security rules review + performance optimization testing (Iteration 21). Tested all manager and referee flows after (1) fetchAppData 1-read optimization, (2) useRealtimeNotifications orderBy+limit, (3) useRealtimeMessages orderBy+limit, and (4) firestore.indexes.json creation. All functional flows pass. Two expected Firestore index errors confirmed (composite indexes not yet deployed to Firebase).

ui_bugs.issue: Toggle buttons lack role='switch' and aria-checked attributes (carry-over from iteration_20 — LOW priority accessibility)

integration_issues.issue: KNOWN/EXPECTED: onSnapshot listener fails with 'The query requires an index' because Firestore composite index notifications(recipient_id ASC, created_at DESC) has not been deployed. New notifications do NOT appear in real-time; page refresh required.

KNOWN/EXPECTED: onSnapshot listener fails with 'The query requires an index' because Firestore composite index messages(participants CONTAINS, created_at DESC) has not been deployed. New messages do NOT appear in real-time; page refresh required.

action_items: CRITICAL (Firebase deployment): Deploy firestore.indexes.json to Firebase to enable realtime sorting. Use 'firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes' or click the index creation URLs embedded in the console errors. Until deployed, realtime listeners fail and users must refresh the page to see new notifications/messages.
LOW: Add role='switch' and aria-checked attributes to custom toggle buttons in NotificationsSettings.jsx for accessibility
critical_code_review_comments: fetchAppData (firestoreService.js lines 128-130): ONE getDocs on users collection correctly replaces 3 separate role-filtered queries. allUsers is split by role in JS. OPTIMIZATION CONFIRMED.
useRealtimeNotifications.js: orderBy('created_at','desc') + limit(100) correct. Fails at runtime due to missing composite index — this is expected until firestore.indexes.json is deployed.
useRealtimeMessages.js: orderBy('created_at','desc') + limit(100) on messages(participants array-contains) correct. Fails at runtime due to missing composite index — expected until deployed.

firestore.indexes.json: Correct composite indexes defined for notifications(recipient_id+created_at) and messages(participants+created_at). Deploy this file to Firebase to fix realtime listener failures.
IMPACT: Without deployed indexes, the initial fetchAppData still works (JS-side sorting in fetchAppData fallback). Users see sorted data on page load but NOT in real-time. No data loss or crashes.
usersMapRef pattern in useRealtimeMessages.js (line 38-41): Correct pattern — avoids re-subscribing on usersMap changes while keeping closure fresh. No bugs.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all UI flows pass; 2 realtime listeners fail with expected index errors requiring Firebase deployment)

test_credentials: Manager: manager@demo.com / manager123 | Referee: referee@demo.com / Referee123
seed_data_creation: Test message sent from manager to referee: 'Manager Index Test Message'
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is pure React + Firebase Vite app at /app (no FastAPI). Iteration 21 tested: (1) fetchAppData 1-read optimization - CONFIRMED working. (2) Realtime notifications/messages with orderBy+limit - CODE CORRECT but fail at runtime because Firestore composite indexes in firestore.indexes.json are NOT yet deployed to Firebase. These are the only 2 console errors visible. All manager tabs (Tournaments, Assignments, Availability, Leaderboard, Roster, Reports) PASS. All referee flows (Dashboard, Messages, Notifications, Calendar+Add Availability, Settings, Payments) PASS. Notification panel sorts newest-first. Messages inbox sorts newest-first. Availability Saved toast appears. Next agent should verify AFTER indexes are deployed: real-time notifications and messages update without page refresh.

rca_of_issue.issue: useRealtimeNotifications and useRealtimeMessages fail with 'The query requires an index'
rca_of_issue.root_cause: Firestore composite queries using orderBy on a filtered field require a composite index to be created in Firebase Console. The code correctly defines these indexes in firestore.indexes.json but the file must be DEPLOYED to Firebase using 'firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes'. The indexes have NOT been deployed yet.
reproduction_steps: 3. See errors: '[useRealtimeNotifications] Listener error: FirebaseError: The query requires an index' and '[useRealtimeMessages] Listener error'
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Deploy the firestore.indexes.json file to Firebase: run 'firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes' from the project root, OR click the Firebase Console direct links in the console error messages to create each index individually. After deployment, the realtime listeners will work.
rca_of_issue.impact: Medium — app is functional (data loads on page refresh) but real-time updates are broken for notifications and messages
